heading: Rules applicable in determining dividends eligible for dividends paid deduction

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: For purposes of this part, the term “dividend” shall, except as otherwise provided in this section, include only dividends described in section 316 (relating to definition of dividends for purposes of corporate distributions).

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of amounts distributed in liquidation, the part of such distribution which is properly chargeable to earnings and profits accumulated after , shall be treated as a dividend for purposes of computing the dividends paid deduction, andFebruary 28, 1913

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a complete liquidation occurring within 24 months after the adoption of a plan of liquidation, any distribution within such period pursuant to such plan shall, to the extent of the earnings and profits (computed without regard to capital losses) of the corporation for the taxable year in which such distribution is made, be treated as a dividend for purposes of computing the dividends paid deduction.

In the case of a complete liquidation of a personal holding company, occurring within 24 months after the adoption of a plan of liquidation, the amount of any distribution within such period pursuant to such plan shall be treated as a dividend for purposes of computing the dividends paid deduction, to the extent that such amount is distributed to corporate distributees and represents such corporate distributees’ allocable share of the undistributed personal holding company income for the taxable year of such distribution computed without regard to this paragraph and without regard to subparagraph (B) of section 316(b)(2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except in the case of a publicly offered regulated investment company (as defined in section 67(c)(2)(B)) or a publicly offered REIT, the amount of any distribution shall not be considered as a dividend for purposes of computing the dividends paid deduction, unless such distribution is pro rata, with no preference to any share of stock as compared with other shares of the same class, and with no preference to one class of stock as compared with another class except to the extent that the former is entitled (without reference to waivers of their rights by shareholders) to such preference. In the case of a distribution by a regulated investment company (other than a publicly offered regulated investment company (as so defined)) to a shareholder who made an initial investment of at least $10,000,000 in such company, such distribution shall not be treated as not being pro rata or as being preferential solely by reason of an increase in the distribution by reason of reductions in administrative expenses of the company.

children.heading: Publicly offered REIT
children.label: 2 Publicly offered REIT
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “publicly offered REIT” means a real estate investment trust which is required to file annual and periodic reports with the Securities and Exchange Commission under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.

children.heading: Distributions by a member of an affiliated group
children.label: d Distributions by a member of an affiliated group
children.text: In the case where a corporation which is a member of an affiliated group of corporations filing or required to file a consolidated return for a taxable year is required to file a separate personal holding company schedule for such taxable year, a distribution by such corporation to another member of the affiliated group shall be considered as a dividend for purposes of computing the dividends paid deduction if such distribution would constitute a dividend under the other provisions of this section to a recipient which is not a member of an affiliated group.

children.heading: Special rules for real estate investment trusts
children.label: e Special rules for real estate investment trusts

children.heading: Determination of earnings and profits for purposes of dividends paid deduction
children.label: 1 Determination of earnings and profits for purposes of dividends paid deduction
A
children.text: the earnings and profits of such trust for any taxable year (but not its accumulated earnings) shall be increased by the amount of gain (if any) on the sale or exchange of real property which is taken into account in determining the taxable income of such trust for such taxable year (and not otherwise taken into account in determining such earnings and profits), and

children.label: B
children.text: section 857(d)(1) shall be applied without regard to subparagraph (B) thereof.

children.heading: Authority to provide alternative remedies for certain failures
children.label: 2 Authority to provide alternative remedies for certain failures
A
children.text: the Secretary determines that such failure is inadvertent or is due to reasonable cause and not due to willful neglect, or

children.label: B
children.text: such failure is of a type of failure which the Secretary has identified for purposes of this paragraph as being described in subparagraph (A).
